title: Zhuangzi, Chapter 31: The Old Fisherman
description: Chapter 31 of the Zhuangzi — the Taoist masterpiece of paradox and parable.
introduction: Chapter 31 of the Zhuangzi — the Taoist masterpiece of paradox and parable.

translation.text: Confucius, after wandering through the Black Curtain Forest, sat down to rest on the Apricot Altar. While the disciples turned to their books, he strummed his lute and sang. He had not gotten halfway through the piece he was playing when an old fisherman stepped out of a boat and came toward him.
